title: AI Fitness Coach Platform
description: An interactive AI-powered workout generator and nutrition planning system offering conversational coaching, health integrations, and progression modeling.
problemSolved: Personalized fitness and nutrition advice is expensive and rarely adaptive. Standard apps offer generic static plans that do not adjust to real-time biofeedback or user constraints (injuries, equipment availability).
architecture: Mobile client (React Native) -> REST API Gateway -> FastAPI Backend -> LangChain Agent -> LLM (Claude & OpenAI) -> VectorDB (Pinecone for contextual memory) & MongoDB (User profiles and workout history).
features: Dynamic AI workout generation based on daily biomechanical feedback
Real-time AI nutrition recommendation and meal adjustment via macro tracking
Conversational AI coaching using personalized multi-modal LLM memory
Apple Health and Google Fit biometric data synchronization
Interactive 3D model visualizations of exercise forms
challenges: Providing consistent and safe coaching advice without hallucinations. Generating structurally sound nutrition plans that strictly respect dietary restrictions.
solutions: Implemented rigorous validation using guardrails (Guardrails AI) and output structured JSON schemas. Designed an agentic RAG system retrieving validated medical-sports guidelines.
results: Delivered 12,000+ custom workout programs with a 92% user adherence rate. Response latency for conversational queries was reduced to 340ms using token streaming.
lessonsLearned: Standard RAG yields poor sports science results. Incorporating a dynamic rule engine that pre-filters vector index queries by biometrics is crucial to preventing athletic injuries.

title: RistoAI - Restaurant Inventory Management
description: An automated invoice processing and inventory tracking platform powered by OCR and Generative AI, delivering real-time cost analysis and supplier metrics.
problemSolved: Restaurant operators waste dozens of hours copying item names, unit quantities, and tax figures from handwritten, crumpled, or poorly lit invoices into outdated inventory systems.
architecture: Document Upload -> OpenCV skew/deskew filters -> LayoutLMv3 Segmenter -> Tesseract OCR Engine -> GPT-4o Pydantic Extraction API -> MongoDB.
features: Instant invoice OCR uploading with LayoutLM text structure extraction
Automatic item classification and matching against current stock database
Live ingredient food cost analysis and markup recommendations
Predictive supplier price tracking and threshold alerts
Automated weekly purchase order generation
challenges: Poor document quality (handwritten annotations, greasy stains, creased folds) causing standard OCR engines to read numbers incorrectly (e.g., misreading '8' as '0' or 'B').
solutions: Developed a hybrid OCR pipeline using OpenCV for preprocessing (deskewing, contrast enhancement) combined with GPT-4o for multi-modal text reconstruction and semantic validation.
results: Achieved a 98.4% extraction accuracy rate across 10,000+ test receipts, reducing average restaurant invoice processing times from 15 minutes to under 8 seconds.
lessonsLearned: Preprocessing (contrast stretching, adaptive thresholding) is 80% of the battle in OCR. Combining raw text coordinates with a semantic LLM parser produces far better results than heuristic rule-based templates.

title: AgentHub - Multi-Agent AI Platform
description: An enterprise-grade orchestration workspace enabling users to deploy, monitor, and link autonomous multi-agent systems with hierarchical memory and RAG engines.
problemSolved: Traditional single-agent setups run sequentially and fail when faced with non-deterministic sub-tasks (e.g. searching the web while debugging code). Context windows easily overflow, and loops are hard to break without state tracking.
architecture: React Frontend -> WebSockets Gateway -> FastAPI Orchestration Engine -> LangGraph State Graph Manager -> Collaborative LLMs (Gemini & OpenAI) -> VectorDB Context Retrievers.
features: Visual drag-and-drop builder for custom agent topologies
State-preserving multi-agent communication graph (supervisor-worker models)
Adaptive RAG ingestion supporting semantic search over documents and web APIs
Shared long-term vector database memory and short-term windowed memory
Real-time stream logging of agent thoughts, tools, and token metrics
challenges: Managing infinite loop conditions where research and coding agents keep debugging back-and-forth without converging, causing runaway token billing.
solutions: Utilized LangGraph's state graph compilation with strict checkpointers and implemented timeout limits, circuit-breaker nodes, and asynchronous token-streaming WebSockets.
results: Reduces execution time for multi-step data tasks by 45% compared to linear chain-of-thought implementations. Built-in checker nodes validate scripts before run, cutting token consumption by 30%.
lessonsLearned: Strict schema constraints on tool calls and explicit 'human-in-the-loop' interrupts for terminal actions are absolutely critical to maintaining control and safety in autonomous systems.

title: Construction Safety Monitoring System
description: A real-time safety compliance monitoring system using YOLO object detection models to flag PPE violations in live video feeds.
problemSolved: Safety officers cannot watch dozens of construction site CCTV streams continuously. Delays in identifying missing helmets or vests lead directly to compliance penalties and high injury rates.
architecture: RTSP CCTV Feeds -> OpenCV Decoders -> YOLOv8 Detection -> ByteTrack Tracker -> Shapely Geofencing logic -> Slack Webhooks API.
features: Real-time PPE detection (safety helmets, high-visibility vests, safety goggles)
Geofenced exclusion zone violation monitoring
Instant push notifications and violation screenshots sent to safety supervisors
Analytics dashboard showing daily compliance scores and historical trends
Multi-camera RTSP stream ingestion at 30 FPS with edge acceleration support
challenges: Silhouettes of workers overlap (occlusion) in crowded areas, and objects like helmets appear tiny when viewed from distant security cameras.
solutions: Fine-tuned YOLOv8 on custom construction site datasets. Implemented tracking-by-detection using ByteTrack to maintain detection status over consecutive frames, reducing false alerts by 85%.
results: Maintains 28 frames per second (FPS) throughput on edge NVIDIA Jetson hardware. ByteTrack tracking keeps worker IDs constant, dropping false violation alerts by 85%.
lessonsLearned: Training on site-specific, low-angle datasets is far more effective than scaling generic COCO datasets. Adding a temporal tracking layer prevents flickering detection boxes from firing duplicate alarms.

title: AI Product Content Generator
description: An automated e-commerce optimization tool that generates titles, descriptions, enhanced product images, and SEO tags from a single photo.
problemSolved: Uploading hundreds of products to Shopify or Amazon requires copywriting and image editing that takes hours per product. Small businesses lack resource to write high-ranking SEO copy.
architecture: Web frontend -> FastAPI Backend -> Multi-Modal Vision LLM -> GPT-4 Text Generation Engine -> Stable Diffusion Image Inpainting -> Shopify API Integrator.
features: Visual product understanding (auto-detects color, style, materials, category)
High-converting SEO product title and description generation based on keyword targets
Automated image enhancement (background removal, shadow insertion, scene context generation)
Meta tags and search schema structured JSON generation
One-click sync to Shopify, WooCommerce, and Amazon Seller Central
challenges: Generating realistic product backgrounds without morphing or modifying the product itself.
solutions: Implemented ControlNet in the image generation pipeline to lock the structural edges of the source product while diffusing new backgrounds into the masked outer boundary.
results: Decreased product listing creation time by 90% for pilot merchant group. Generated SEO copy showed a 14% uplift in organic search click-through rate.
lessonsLearned: ControlNet is essential; standard visual prompts for SD image diffusion skew the shapes of products, making details inaccurate for customers.
